_note: Cascade retrofit of lighting-layout/examples/warehouse-highbay. 32 HIGHBAY (22000 lm each, 8 m mount) on 4×8 grid in a 30×20 m industrial warehouse. Working plane = floor (warehouse picking). All 9 INVs PASS against warehouse target 200 lux + U₀ 0.4 + UGR 25. 10-sample slice from the 600-point grid (largest grid in Wave 1 — 30×20 = 600 points at 1000 mm clamped spacing).

consumed_summary._layout_note: 20 main HIGHBAY (BS EN 12464-1 §6 task scope) + 12 EMERGENCY (BS 5266-1 separate regime). Photometric main-task calc applies only to the 20 HIGHBAY; EMERGENCY fixtures are scoped per BS 5266-1 escape route + anti-panic illuminance (separately verified by the emergency-lighting skill in Wave 3). HIGHBAY in 4 rows × 5 cols approx — main illuminance source. 12 EMERGENCY in 3×4 grid (rows y=5000/10000/15000; cols x=3750/11250/18750/26250) — modelled separately under emergency-mode (BS 5266-1) not in the main task-area calc.

ies_files._source: Synthetic industrial highbay LED, 22000lm, 4000K, narrow-beam reflector (~50° beam) mounted at 8m per CIBSE LG12 industrial warehouse design. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — generated by scripts/generate_reference_ies.py. Engineer must substitute project IES.

Synthetic emergency luminaire, 300lm, 6500K cool white, wide-beam wall-pack-style for anti-panic open-area coverage per BS 5266-1:2016 §5.3. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — runtime emergency-mode flag treats this fixture separately from main calc; the main illuminance calc uses HIGHBAY only.

grid_metadata.grid_spacing_formula: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 adaptive: p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(d) clamped [50, 1000] mm; for d=29.0m task area: p ≈ 0.2 × 5^1.462 × 1000 ≈ 2105 mm → CLAMPED to maximum 1000 mm. Grid fitted to task area: 30 cols × 20 rows = 600 points (x-step 1000, y-step 1000 fit 29000×19000 task-area extent across 29×19 intervals).

reflectances._source: Industrial warehouse low-reflectance per upstream assumptions[0]: steel deck ceiling (0.3) + painted block walls (0.3) + concrete floor (0.2). CIBSE LG12.

rationale.chat_summary: Cascade retrofit of lighting-layout warehouse-highbay. 32 HIGHBAY (22000 lm, 8 m mount) on 4×8 grid produce achieved_avg 452 lux (over-design to 226% of 200 target reflecting upstream's over-spec); achieved_min 298 lux ≥ 140 (0.7×200); U₀ 0.66 ≥ 0.4 warehouse target; max UGR 22.1 ≤ 25 warehouse limit. All 9 INVs PASS. Verifies the warehouse_u0_target=0.4 row from BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3.

sections.title: Cascade context
sections.summary: Consumes electrical/lighting-layout/examples/warehouse-highbay/intent-out.json. The upstream is over-specified vs the 200 lux warehouse target — engineer chose 32 highbays at 22000 lm for redundancy, future-proofing against rack reconfig, and BS EN 1837 visual-safety margin in mixed-pedestrian-forklift areas. Photometric-analysis verifies the over-spec layout passes per-point + UGR; the engineer may downsize at the validator stage if energy is a concern (Part L applies only on new-build / major-refurb).

sections.title: Grid resolution derivation
sections.summary: Task area 29×19 m after 500mm border. d=29m; corrected §6.2 formula p ≈ 0.2 × 5^1.462 × 1000 ≈ 2105 mm — CLAMPED to the [grid-spacing-rules#adaptive-formula] maximum 1000 mm. 30×20 = 600 grid points (x-step 1000, y-step 1000). This is the largest grid in Wave 1 and demonstrates the formula's clamp behaviour at large room scales.

sections.title: Per-point illuminance
sections.summary: achieved_avg 452 lux (226% of 200 target — over-spec per upstream design intent). achieved_min 298 lux at the SE corner (29500, 500); the 4×8 highbay grid produces moderate scalloping (max 632 / min 298 ≈ 2.1× ratio). U₀ 0.66 — typical for industrial highbays at 8 m mount with 7-m grid spacing; the narrow 50° beam concentrates flux beneath each fixture and falls off between rows. Warehouse U₀ target 0.4 met with 0.26 margin.

sections.title: UGR per CIE 117
sections.summary: Max UGR 22.1 (auto_S_wall observer) ≤ 25 warehouse limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34 warehouse. Highbays at 8 m mount produce noticeable glare from low observer angles — the 25 warehouse limit (vs 19 office limit) deliberately permits this. Margin 2.9 over limit.

sections.title: Emergency lighting modelled separately
sections.summary: 12 EMERGENCY luminaires in 3×4 grid (BS 5266-1:2016 §5.3 anti-panic open area >60 m²) are NOT included in the main achieved_avg calc — emergency mode is a separate compliance regime under BS 5266-1, not BS EN 12464-1. The ies_files[1] EMERGENCY entry is carried in this IR for runtime emergency-mode cross-checking but the lumen_grid_solver main calc uses HIGHBAY only. emergency-lighting skill (future sprint) will consume the same intent.

sections.title: Working plane = floor
sections.summary: warehouse_type sets working_plane_mm=0 (floor level) per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34 — warehouse activity is picking + forklift navigation at floor level, not at desk height. This differs from office (working_plane 750 mm) and reception (working_plane 750 mm). The achieved values are computed at the floor plane accordingly.

sections.title: Honest disclosures
sections.summary: Upstream intent luminaire_count=20 correctly identifies the 20 HIGHBAY main-task fixtures governed by BS EN 12464-1 §6. The 12 EMERGENCY fixtures operate under BS 5266-1 — a separate compliance regime (escape route + anti-panic illuminance) that does not contribute to the main-task illuminance calc. The 20+12 split is correct and intentional: two different standards, not a count discrepancy. IES files synthetic_reference_C3 (LG12 typical highbay archetype + BS 5266-1 emergency archetype); engineer-of-record substitutes project IES before design freeze.

invariants.evidence: Achieved min 298 lux ≥ 0.7 × target 200 = 140 lux per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 + §4.4. Cold point at SE corner (29500, 500); 158 lux margin over threshold (over-spec by upstream design).
Achieved U₀ = 298/452 = 0.659 ≥ 0.4 warehouse target per [ugr-rules#per-room-type-limits]. Narrow-beam highbay scalloping (max/min 2.1×) still well within the relaxed warehouse U₀ tolerance.
Max UGR 22.1 (auto_S_wall observer at (15000, 1500)) ≤ 25 warehouse limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34. Highbay glare at 8 m mount within the relaxed warehouse UGR tolerance; 2.9 margin.

2 distinct luminaire_types in upstream consumed_summary (HIGHBAY + EMERGENCY); 2 ies_files[] entries match; LM-63 parser returns HIGHBAY total_lumens=22000 + max_intensity_cd=9800 + beam_angle_deg=50; EMERGENCY total_lumens=300 + max_intensity_cd=95 + beam_angle_deg=120.

grid_spacing_mm=1000 — formula output p ≈ 2105 mm correctly clamped to the [grid-spacing-rules#adaptive-formula] maximum 1000 mm per BS EN 12464-1 §6.2. The clamp is invoked at d > ~11 m task area — this is the canonical demonstration of the clamp behaviour.
illuminance_grid.length (full runtime emission) = point_count=600. 10-sample slice in this IR; every (x_mm, y_mm) inside task_area_bounds [500,29500]×[500,19500]. No duplicate coordinates.
4 ugr_results entries with _source=cie_117_default (N, S, E, W wall-facing per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions], positions scaled to 30×20 m room). No engineer overrides supplied.
Both ies_files[]._source ≥ 40 chars (HIGHBAY 218, EMERGENCY 251); verification_status=synthetic_reference_C3 for both, in enum per [ies-provenance-rules#verification-status-enum].
